game_name: Solar Jetman: Hunt for the Golden Warpship

sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for NES version

special_mechanics.name: Pod Flight Physics
special_mechanics.description: Jetman controls a small jet pod that is subject to inertia but NOT drag physics depending on planet gravity level. The pod maneuvers in clockwise or anti-clockwise directions.

physics_values.notes: Gravity changes every level, making stable flight challenging and maneuvering progressively more difficult

special_mechanics.name: Variable Planetary Gravity
special_mechanics.description: Each of twelve planets (plus one hidden planet) has its own distinct gravitational pull, fundamentally altering the physics behavior of the pod.

special_mechanics.name: Tow Cable / Item Drag
special_mechanics.description: Items are collected via a tow cable. The collected item creates drag that makes flight more difficult and responsive.

special_mechanics.name: Ejection and Suit Gameplay
special_mechanics.description: When a pod is destroyed, Jetman ejects and walks around in a space suit. The suit is described as agile but vulnerable.

tier_notes: Tier 2 (Empirical/Qualitative): No precise numerical physics values are documented in available sources. Game mechanics are described qualitatively emphasizing inertia, variable gravity, and physics-based pod flight without specific acceleration/deceleration values, frame data, or sub-pixel information.
